other_observers: Maggie Boswell, Rachel Hopper, David Waltman, Joe Roller, John Drummond and Gary Matthews.; No, originally found and identified by Glenn Walbek and Loch Kilpatrick. Subsequently seen by Joey Kellner, Bill Schmoker and many others.
specific_location: Marina sand spit at Chatfield State Park.
description: This was a large, but not huge, breeding-plumaged Gull. The head, underparts and tail were pure white. The bill was yellow with a medium sized red spot, and a quite large gonydeal angle. The legs were pink, but not very bright. The mantle was rather dark, but was not black. The bird had a broader than average white tertial crescent. The tips of the primaries on the upper wings were black, plus they were darker than the rest of the underwing. There was a quite large white mirror on P10 only. Most of these features can be seen on the photographs.Behaviors: The bird spent most of its time loafing on the sand spit and feeding from a dead fish on the sand. It stretched its wings several times, and took short flights on two occasions.Call: None heardPlumage: Breeding
photographer: Photographer: Photo by David Waltman, Photo by David Waltman, Photo by David Waltman
